data.title: Theravance Biopharma reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Theravance Biopharma press release (TBPH): Q4 net income was $61.0 million.
  • Revenue of $45.9 million.
  • Cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities totaled $326.5 million as of December 31, 2025.

data.title: ECB officials ready to hike rates if inflation rises too much - report
data.description:
European Central Bank officials would be prepared to raise interest rates as soon as their next meeting should repercussions from the Iran war propel inflation too far above its target, according to a media report on Thursday.

The policymakers kept the bank's key rates unchanged at Thursday's meeting, as expected. If second-round effects fuel inflation, such a move could be made at the April 29-30 meeting, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

No decision has been made, and a later date may be more appropriate, the report noted. Some officials may be hesitant to hike rates at the April meeting, which doesn't include updated forecasts, and may instead see June as more appropriate.

Money market pricing puts a ~60% probability of a quarter-point increase in April, up from 50% earlier, Bloomberg said. Traders expect ECB policymakers to act as attacks in the Gulf cause energy prices to soar with the disruption of supply routes. At least two 25-basis-point increases are priced in for this year.

iShares Europe ETF (IEV) fell 1.0%, and iShares MSCI Europe Financials ETF (EUFN) slipped 1.1% in Thursday afternoon trading. The Invesco CurrencyShares Euro ETF (FXE) rose 0.8%.

  • Accumulated a total of 13,628 bitcoin as of March 17, 2026.

data.title: Revolution, Ascendis, Arrowhead among RBC’s top M&A candidates in biotech
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets has compiled a list of 13 biotechs that are most likely to receive takeover interest, including Revolution Medicines (RVMD), Ascendis Pharma (ASND), and Arrowhead Pharma (ARWR), among notable M&A contenders.

RBC analyst Leonid Timashev based his analysis on biotech deal speculation in the media. Using M&A commentary posted by four leading publications known for their M&A commentary, namely, The Wall Street Journal, The Financial Times, Bloomberg, and Betaville, Timashev found roughly 85 such reports since 2021.

He found a wide variation not only in the “hit rate” of the above publications but also in the returns shareholders could generate after those M&A reports were published.

In terms of hit rates, Betaville trailed at roughly 20%-30%, while others indicated a 60%-70% hit rate. The median return of stories published in Bloomberg and The Financial Times was the highest at 27% over 30 days, while that for the FT increased to 70% over 90 days.

“Given the importance of M&A to the sector, it is not surprising to us that trade publication commentary can drive significant volatility,” Timashev wrote in a research note on Wednesday.

Analysis also looked at the biotechs with the most promising M&A prospects. In addition to Revolution Medicines (RVMD), Ascendis (ASND), and Arrowhead (ARWR), he pointed to Kymera Therapeutics (KYMR), Rhythm Pharmaceuticals (RYTM), Xenon Pharmaceuticals (XENE), Dyne Therapeutics (DYN), and Viridian Therapeutics (DYN) as potential takeover targets.

BioCryst Pharmaceuticals (BCRX), Wave Life Sciences (WVE), Ocular Therapeutix (OCUL), Pharvaris (PHVS), and EyePoint (EYPT) rounded out the target list.

In terms of potential bidders, Timashev pointed to Merck (MRK), AbbVie (ABBV), Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY), Biogen (BIIB), Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX), Regeneron (REGN), Jazz Pharmaceuticals (JAZZ), and Acadia Pharmaceuticals (ACAD) as companies most likely to pursue deal-making.

“For acquirers, discussion of M&A does not drive the same upward inflection as for the smaller-cap biotechs that are often the targets,” the analyst noted, adding that it could be due to concerns over cash outlays, strategic fit, and other unfavorable views around the deal.

“We caveat that this does not mean that these deals, or interest in deals, do not produce long-term value, but rather, short-term investor reactions may often be mixed,” Timashev wrote.

data.title: Goldman Sachs plans small rounds of layoffs beginning April, shifts job cuts strategy - report
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (GS) is planning to begin small rounds of layoffs starting in April, skipping its usual spring "Strategic Resource Assessment," multiple people familiar with the situation told Business Insider.

The banking giant is said to be shifting its strategy regarding headcount reduction. Instead of a single, larger-scale headcount cut, the bank is planning to implement several rounds of cuts.

Goldman Sachs is reportedly giving business-line leaders more discretion to set their timelines.

The New York-headquartered lender's multiple rounds of smaller, rolling cuts are expected to continue through the summer and hit all business lines, the Thursday, March 19, report noted.

The traditional SRA round could still happen later in 2026, according to the people who declined to be identified discussing nonpublic information.

The 2026 reductions are expected to be significantly fewer than the cuts last March, with the final names and numbers for the upcoming rounds yet to be finalized.

data.title: S&P 500 slides but these 15 stocks remain overbought
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) moved lower on Thursday afternoon, falling about 0.5% as investors maintained a cautious, risk-off stance amid escalating tensions in the Middle East. Heightened geopolitical uncertainty has weighed on broader market sentiment, limiting upside momentum for equities.

Despite the broader decline, pockets of strength remain. Technical data shows that 15 S&P 500 constituents currently carry relative strength index readings above 70, signaling overbought conditions. The divergence highlights selective resilience in certain stocks even as overall market sentiment remains subdued. 

Outlined below are the 15 most overbought S&P 500 stocks according to the RSI indicator:

  1. APA Corporation (APA), RSI of 81.73.
  2. EOG Resources (EOG), RSI of 76.54.
  3. Chevron Corporation (CVX), RSI of 75.96.
  4. LyondellBasell Industries (LYB), RSI of 75.22. 
  5. Phillips 66 (PSX), RSI of 74.72.

  7. Valero Energy (VLO), RSI of 73.68.

  9. Marathon Petroleum (MPC), RSI of 73.23.

  11. Ciena Corporation (CIEN), RSI of 72.76. 
  12. Diamondback Energy (FANG), RSI of 72.16.
  13. Coterra Energy (CTRA), RSI of 71.45. 
  14. EQT Corporation (EQT), RSI of 71.32.
  15. Dow Inc. (DOW), RSI of 70.73. 

S&P 500 ETFs: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX).

data.title: Trump administration says not considering oil export ban
data.description:
The Trump administration has no plan to implement restrictions on oil and gas exports, U.S. Energy Secretary Wright said Thursday following a meeting between Vice President Vance and oil executives.

Industry leaders had asked at the meeting directly about whether the administration was weighing limits on crude exports - a move the industry strongly opposes - to tamp down rising prices.

Limiting the U.S. export of refined products such as gasoline and diesel could ensure that more supplies exist for domestic use. Experts have warned such a move could backfire and cause unintended consequences, such as prompting U.S. Gulf Coast refineries to cut production, resulting in higher prices.

"Banning refined products or crude exports would be counterproductive for lowering pump prices, incite panic buying, and trigger further price spikes in global markets," Rapidan Energy president Bob McNally told Bloomberg.

Since lifting a ban on crude oil exports in 2015, the U.S. has emerged as the world's largest oil producer, with shipments often surpassing those of any OPEC nation other than Saudi Arabia.

ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

data.title: Cash Dubai crude surges past $170 per barrel, setting historic record
data.description:
Cash Dubai crude prices shattered records earlier on Thursday, breaking above $170 per barrel for the first time in history.

“Cash Dubai crude (balance of the month) just broke above $170 per barrel,” commodity analyst Rory Johnston said on X during midday trading (New York time). “To my knowledge, no crude has ever commanded more than $170/bbl before.”

The unprecedented price spike signals extreme tightness in oil markets amid the war in Iran. While Johnston noted the supply constraints remain localized for now, he warned the situation could deteriorate further.

“Extreme tightness still local, but it will spread with time,” Johnston said in a follow-up post.

The record-breaking prices across Middle East crude benchmarks come in the wake of mounting concerns over global energy supplies and could have far-reaching consequences for consumers and businesses already grappling with elevated fuel costs.

Oil ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL).

Natural Gas ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), and (UNL).

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC).

data.title: Brad Garlinghouse sees Clarity Act odds at 90%: XRP, BTC, ETH make-or-break?
data.description:
In a recent interview on Fox Business, Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse said there’s a 90% chance the Clarity Act passes by end-April, reported by the analyst Crypto Tice—if it passes, will Bitcoin (BTC-USD), Ethereum (ETH-USD), and XRP (XRP-USD) break out, or will a delay slow the momentum?

Ripple CEO's statement just strengthened what Alex Thorn said earlier. He warned that if the crypto market structure bill isn’t approved by the end of April, its chances in 2026 will drop sharply. These statement alignments add strong backing to the bill’s decision timeline.

XRP (XRP-USD) is trading around ~$1.43 after facing rejection near ~$1.60 levels. As per the chart structure, RSI is near ~37, showing weak momentum due to geopolitical tensions going around. Any positive signal in cryptocurrency’s rules and regulations may push XRP to reclaim ~$1.60. However, the current momentum may drag it toward its major $1.35 support area.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is currently in its bearish behavior, trading near ~$69.3K and facing resistance near ~$70K, as per TradingView chart analysis. If the CLARITY Act passes, a break above $70K can push it directly toward ~$75K, but if it fails, a weak trend and low RSI near ~30 may pull the asset back to the ~$64K zone.

Similarly, Ethereum (ETH-USD) is showing a clear downtrend, trading around ~$2.11K after failing to move above the $2.35K resistance level. Its RSI is near ~27, which is close to oversold, so now only a positive signal by the Senate may push it towards the ~$2.50K mark. But no decision or delay combined with current weakness may drag the asset down to its strongest ~$1.80K support zone.

Currently, crypto markets are at a key turning point, with the U.S. crypto regulation bill acting as a major trigger for the next clear trend. The upcoming month could be make-or-break for the industry.
